name: Green Day
bio: Green Day still earn through one of the strongest modern punk and alternative catalogs, with songs that remain large across streaming, sync, and generational rediscovery.
notable_for: Punk-pop songs with major long-tail replay and cross-era familiarity.
career_highlight: Basket Case and Boulevard of Broken Dreams continue to anchor the catalog's long-tail earnings profile.
revenue_drivers: Alternative and pop-punk playlists sustain recurring listening.
Cross-era hit density supports constant catalog discovery.
Sync use and youth-culture nostalgia reinforce long-tail value.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps Green Day's headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: Green Day's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
